content:
- Finished bug-real-binary-provenance-enforcement-gaps end-to-end.
- Implemented fail-closed real-binary provenance enforcement via repo-tracked policy (tools/real-binaries-policy.json) + local attestation (.tools/real-binaries-attestation.json) and new verifier (src/test_harness/provenance.rs).
- Hardened installers:
  - tools/install-common.sh provides hardened command resolution (allowlist + validated symlink targets, preserves argv[0] semantics), atomic copy helper (used for etcd), and lock-protected attestation manifest merge.
  - tools/install-etcd.sh now uses hardened command paths, stricter curl flags, mktemp, and writes attestation with resolved_path_abs.
  - tools/install-postgres16.sh reverted to allowlisted symlink strategy for /usr/bin/* (copying broke initdb due to share/libdir relocation); adds rpm -V integrity check and writes attestation with resolved_path_abs.
- Updated harness to use provenance verifier for real-binary paths (src/test_harness/binaries.rs) and added deterministic negative-control unit tests for tampering.
- Added tools/trace-real-binary-execve.sh and committed execve evidence summary under .ralph/evidence/.../execve-summary.txt.
- Updated contributor docs (docs/src/contributors/testing-system.md, harness-internals.md) with provenance model + commands.
- Verified gates all green: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.
- Switched task via .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed + pushed.

- Read previous progress and scanned .ralph/current_tasks.md for failing/meta tasks.
- Confirmed all bug tasks are passing; identified remaining non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes> (container-first deployment, docs expansion, pgBackRest backup/recovery story).
- Selected next task: start pgBackRest backup/recovery story at step 01 (provider abstraction + pgBackRest default) and wrote its path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Read docs surfaces: docs/src/operator/configuration.md and quick-start pages (prerequisites, first-run, initial-validation).
- Verified current docs only list postgres/pg_ctl/pg_rewind/initdb/pg_basebackup/psql and do not mention pgbackrest or a backup provider block.
- Cross-checked src/config/schema.rs and defaults/parser quickly: current committed code still has no backup config model yet, only process.binaries with six tools.
- Located task spec for story-pgbackrest task 01; it defines intended docs changes: required process.binaries.pgbackrest and explicit backup block with provider/stanza/repo/options defaults.
- Next step after this response: either apply a focused docs patch with minimal operator wording once the exact schema field names are finalized, or proceed now with placeholder-safe wording explicitly marked as schema-pending.

- Investigated real-binary prerequisite pipeline end-to-end for user request: read src/test_harness/binaries.rs, src/test_harness/provenance.rs, tools/install-common.sh, tools/install-etcd.sh, tools/install-postgres16.sh, tools/real-binaries-policy.json, and docs provenance sections.
- Confirmed current behavior details:
  - binaries.rs delegates real test binary resolution to provenance::require_verified_real_binary labels (etcd + postgres tool set), then builds BinaryPaths for process fixtures.
  - install-etcd.sh installs pinned v3.6.8 archive by arch with strict SHA256 verification and attests etcd entry.
  - install-postgres16.sh installs via dnf module stream 16, verifies rpm package integrity, resolves binaries from allowlisted system prefixes, symlinks into .tools/postgres16/bin, then attests each binary entry.
  - install-common.sh enforces command-path allowlists, and generates/merges attestation entries atomically with flock.
  - provenance.rs verifies policy+attestation fail-closed: schema version, path constraints, symlink policy, perms, sha256+size, expected version; caches verified map in OnceLock.
- Found doc surfaces requiring updates when adding pgbackrest: quick-start prerequisites, contributors testing-system/harness-internals install commands and provenance text.
- Noted implementation impact for adding pgbackrest path into BinaryPaths is broad: config schema/parser/defaults validation + many RuntimeConfig/BinaryPaths fixture constructors + ha_e2e startup payload projection + process worker real config helpers.
- Extra host check: dnf info pgbackrest currently returns no matching package on this AlmaLinux 9 env, so installer design should either bootstrap the proper repo explicitly or fail with actionable repo-enable instructions rather than assuming package availability.
- Next: deliver a concrete proposal for adding pgbackrest prerequisite + tools/install-pgbackrest.sh + provenance policy updates + fixture reference strategy and sequencing (without code edits this turn).

- Scanned the v2 config pipeline end-to-end: schema types, defaults normalization, parser normalization, runtime validation, and test fixtures.
- Confirmed v2 entrypoint is RuntimeConfigV2Input in src/config/schema.rs, normalized by normalize_runtime_config_v2 in src/config/parser.rs, with safe defaults from src/config/defaults.rs and strict post-normalization validation in validate_runtime_config.
- Located process binaries definition in schema BinaryPaths and path validation in parser validate_runtime_config for postgres/pg_ctl/pg_rewind/initdb/pg_basebackup/psql.
- Mapped all compile-impact sites for BinaryPaths literals and RuntimeConfig literals across src/, tests/, and examples; also found TOML fixture binaries objects in parser tests + docs.
- Traced real-binary harness requirements: src/test_harness/binaries.rs currently requires postgres toolchain binaries but not pgbackrest; tools/real-binaries-policy.json and tools/install-postgres16.sh also currently omit pgbackrest.
- Confirmed only one JSON-shaped runtime config payload with explicit process.binaries keys exists in src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs (for dcs.init payload), and it must be updated if BinaryPaths grows.
- Next step: provide user a precise edit plan including exact struct additions for backup block + defaulting and validation strategy, and explicit test/fixture file update list for pgbackrest + backup fields.

- Inspected process job system core: src/process/jobs.rs, state.rs, worker.rs, plus runtime startup integration and HA dispatch callsites.
- Confirmed production command runner is TokioCommandRunner in process/worker.rs; startup path in runtime/node.rs also uses same runner + same ProcessCommandSpec build_command function.
- Mapped typing/correlation: ProcessJobKind in process/state.rs; ActiveJobKind in process/jobs.rs; correlation mainly via JobId in request/active/outcome/log attributes. HA composes job ids from tick/index/action/time; startup currently uses startup-<pid> id.
- Mapped command construction: build_command match branches per job kind; role auth env only for basebackup/rewind via PGPASSWORD secret source; secrets resolved at spawn time and trailing newlines trimmed.
- Mapped capture behavior: capture_output toggles piped stdio; output drained line-wise each tick and logged with PgTool producer, ChildStdout/ChildStderr transport, and attributes job_id/job_kind/binary (+raw_bytes_hex for non-UTF8).
- Checked structured output patterns: subprocess worker logs are raw lines + attributes; no parsed structured payload in JobOutcome. Postgres log ingest has JSON/plain normalization pattern and archive wrapper emits JSON lines with domain payload.
- Noted extension constraints for backup-provider jobs: single-active process worker queue (Busy rejections), no published last_rejection in ProcessState, no state payload channel for info/check outputs.
- Next useful step: implement new backup-provider job/spec enums and build_command branches, then decide whether info/check need only logs or typed parsed result payloads in ProcessState/JobOutcome.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and loaded `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/01-task-backup-provider-abstraction-and-pgbackrest-default.md`.
- Spawned parallel explorer agents to scan: config v2 schema/parser/defaults patterns; process job system; test harness provenance + installers; docs update touchpoints.
- Wrote a full, file-by-file execution plan into the task file, including: new `crate::backup` module design, config schema changes (`process.binaries.pgbackrest` + `backup` block), process job kinds/specs for backup operations, harness/provenance policy + installer (`tools/install-pgbackrest.sh`), docs updates, and required gate commands.
- Added TO BE VERIFIED marker and intentionally stopped before implementation.
- Next step: perform deep skeptical review of the plan (must change at least one design choice), then flip marker to NOW EXECUTE.

- Reviewed plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/01-task-backup-provider-abstraction-and-pgbackrest-default.md` for scope and AC consistency.
- Identified three high-confidence execution risks: an over-broad required test gate, a likely gap between intended abstract API usage and actual wiring, and a config validation mismatch when backup is disabled.
- Did not edit code; only performed review and produced risk-ranked recommendations.
- Next: if we proceed, apply one of the three edits below (in this order: scope gate, call-site wiring, validation semantics) before implementation work starts.

- Reviewed task file .ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/01-task-backup-provider-abstraction-and-pgbackrest-default.md with focus on config schema/parser/default patterns.
- Inspected src/config/schema.rs, src/config/defaults.rs, src/config/parser.rs and confirmed v2 normalization pattern: RuntimeConfigV2Input -> normalize_* helpers -> validate_runtime_config.
- Noted required-field handling split: Option<T> wrappers for many v2 secure blocks (roles/tls/identities etc) gives actionable ConfigError::Validation field paths, but process.binaries currently uses Option<BinaryPaths> where BinaryPaths leaves are hard-required serde fields (no per-leaf V2 wrapper).
- Identified risk that adding process.binaries.pgbackrest directly to BinaryPaths can yield parse-time missing-field errors instead of actionable Validation and can destabilize parser tests expecting validation-field behavior.
- Mapped fixture blast radius for new required fields: many RuntimeConfig/BinaryPaths literals across src/tests/examples plus many inline TOML fixtures in parser tests and JSON dcs.init payload in src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs that must decode as RuntimeConfig.
- Suggested concrete plan change: add BinaryPathsV2Input + normalize_binary_paths_v2 path to preserve v2 actionable missing-field semantics for pgbackrest and future binary additions, plus explicit test for missing process.binaries.pgbackrest returning ConfigError::Validation field process.binaries.pgbackrest.

- Reviewed active plan file .ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/01-task-backup-provider-abstraction-and-pgbackrest-default.md with focus on section 3 process integration.
- Deep-checked src/process/jobs.rs, src/process/state.rs, src/process/worker.rs patterns: one active job at a time, ProcessJobKind->ActiveJobKind mirror mapping, timeout_for_kind config fallbacks, build_command validation+arg construction, stdout/stderr logging attributes, and existing unit+real-binary tests.
- Found mismatches in the plan: (1) tests too weak ("at least one provider op") compared to existing per-job pattern, (2) timeout default strategy for backup ops unspecified even though process config uses explicit timeout buckets, (3) command renderer boundary risks duplicating log_identity/program concerns that currently live in worker build_command, (4) parser-only option validation is insufficient because ProcessJobRequest can be created internally bypassing parser.
- Proposed better approach: add explicit backup timeout config field(s), strengthen process tests to full operation matrix + lifecycle + real-binary smoke, keep worker as owner of ProcessCommandSpec/log identity while backup renderer returns operation args/env fragment, and enforce option validation at command-build time (defense in depth).
- Optional extra risk note for future backup worker: current Busy rejections are internal (last_rejection not published), so auto-scheduling should be deferred or rejection surfacing added before enqueuing periodic backup jobs.
- Next immediate step (if asked): patch the plan doc to encode these changes and mark the altered plan item explicitly.

- Reviewed harness/provenance/tooling files for adding required pgbackrest binary: src/test_harness/binaries.rs, src/test_harness/provenance.rs, tools/install-common.sh, tools/install-etcd.sh, tools/install-postgres16.sh, tools/install-mdbook*.sh, tools/real-binaries-policy.json.
- Confirmed current wiring: binaries.rs sources required process binaries via provenance labels (postgres, pg_ctl, pg_rewind, initdb, pg_basebackup, psql) and no pgbackrest mapping yet.
- Found key provenance constraint: ExpectedVersion only supports etcd/postgres kinds; executables require expected_version; pgbackrest cannot be represented today without extending enum + verify_version parser.
- Found policy/symlink coupling: install-postgres16 links .tools/postgres16/bin/* to system binaries; provenance allows leaf symlink only when allowed_target_prefixes exists. A new pgbackrest policy entry must include allowlist prefixes if installed as symlink.
- Found stale guidance pitfall for new installer script: provenance error messages hardcode running ./tools/install-etcd.sh and ./tools/install-postgres16.sh in several spots; adding install-pgbackrest.sh requires updating those strings (and docs).
- Verified local package availability risk: `dnf info pgbackrest` returns “No matching Packages to list” on this AlmaLinux/RHEL-like environment, so installer must either enable explicit repo or use pinned archive/source flow; cannot assume default repos.
- Extra note: pinned_archive is currently only deserialized and not actively verified by provenance runtime, so policy pin metadata is informational today.
- Next step done in response: provide exact add sequence and one improvement proposal (centralized versions manifest to eliminate policy/script drift).

- Handled PO email "What is going on" as a direct investigation task (no code changes).
- Sent frequent status emails via receive_mail/reply.sh: ack start, plan/update, findings, and final closeout.
- Checked runtime status from receive_mail side: `agent-status.sh` and `process_registry.json` show process `sapphire-swift` active with subject "What is going on".
- Verified service details with `systemctl --user status agent-sapphire-swift.service`; unit is active/running and hosts codex process.
- Verified log movement in `/home/joshazimullah.linux/work_mounts/patroni_rewrite/receive_mail/output.log` by size/mtime delta over 3s and poll counter delta over 4s: `POLL #82647` -> `POLL #82651`.
- Interpreted current behavior from tail: repeated IMAP NOOP keepalive + UID checks + "No new emails" loop, plus command execution event records while processing this request.
- Also checked `.ralph/ralph-status.sh`: `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` active; event-watch compatibility service inactive with known missing ExecStart target warning.
- Next step if asked: provide targeted journal slice for `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` (time-bounded) to describe what that worker is doing in more detail.
